chunk_summary: Defines routine indicators with numerators and denominators for monitoring pediatric nutrition programs, emphasizing measurable fractions for corrective actions and operational examples like growth monitoring, THR continuity, and PM POSHAN meal-days.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of defining routine indicators with explicit numerators and denominators in pediatric nutrition programs.
Learn to translate program questions into measurable fractions for effective monitoring and evaluation.
Familiarize with operational examples such as growth monitoring coverage, THR continuity, and PM POSHAN meal-days.
Recognize the need for quality checks when interpreting outcome indicators from routine data.
key_facts: Growth monitoring coverage (0–3 years): Numerator = children weighed; Denominator = expected children to be weighed.
THR continuity: Numerator = beneficiaries receiving full ration; Denominator = registered eligible beneficiaries.
PM POSHAN meal-days: Numerator = school days with meals served; Denominator = eligible school days.
NDD coverage: Numerator = children 1–19 years swallowing albendazole; Denominator = total enrolled children 1–19 years.
WIFS adherence proxy: Numerator = adolescents consuming weekly IFA dose; Denominator = enrolled adolescents scheduled for the dose.
reference_quote: Teach residents to translate each program question into a measurable fraction and to record the data source and frequency.
document_key_topics: Routine indicator definitions for pediatric nutrition programs
Importance of data quality in outcome interpretation
